skill.name: umap-learn
skill.description: Guidance for applying UMAP dimensionality reduction to visualization, clustering preprocessing, supervised embeddings, parametric UMAP, and model pipelines.
skill.summary: Use UMAP to reduce high-dimensional data for visualization, clustering, and machine learning workflows.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command execution and blocker patterns, but review found Markdown code fences, inline API notation, and ordinary UMAP documentation. No evidence found of prompt injection, secret access, data exfiltration, hidden network behavior, or executable automation in the two scanned files.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Findings Are Markdown Examples
low_findings.description: False positive. The external command detections occur in Markdown code fences and inline Python or API examples. The skill documents UMAP usage and does not execute commands itself.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are fenced Markdown examples or inline documentation. No runnable wrapper, script hook, or dynamic command invocation is present.

low_findings.title: Static Blocker Findings Are Benign Text Matches
low_findings.description: False positive. The weak cryptography, system reconnaissance, and network reconnaissance detections map to UMAP description text and numerical examples, not security-sensitive behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines describe dimensionality reduction concepts and array grid construction. They do not access networks, inspect systems, or implement cryptography.

content.value_statement: High-dimensional datasets are hard to inspect, cluster, and reuse in models. This skill gives practical UMAP guidance for embeddings, parameter tuning, visualization, and ML preprocessing.
actual_capabilities: Explains when to use UMAP for visualization, clustering, feature engineering, and supervised embeddings.
Provides parameter guidance for n_neighbors, min_dist, n_components, metrics, and reproducibility settings.
Describes preprocessing steps such as feature scaling before fitting UMAP models.
Covers HDBSCAN-oriented workflows that use UMAP embeddings before density-based clustering.
Documents advanced options including Parametric UMAP, inverse transforms, DensMAP, and AlignedUMAP.
limitations: It does not run UMAP automatically or inspect datasets without user-provided code and data.
It cannot guarantee clusters are real; users must validate embeddings with domain checks and metrics.
It depends on the installed umap-learn, scikit-learn, plotting, and optional TensorFlow packages.
It is reference guidance, not a replacement for statistical review of model results.

use_cases.title: Explore Complex Research Data
use_cases.description: Reduce genomics, image, sensor, or embedding features into two or three dimensions for visual inspection.

use_cases.title: Prepare Features for Clustering
use_cases.description: Tune UMAP embeddings before HDBSCAN or related clustering methods while avoiding visualization-only settings.

use_cases.title: Build Reusable ML Pipelines
use_cases.description: Apply UMAP transformations to train and test data with reproducible parameters and scikit-learn conventions.

prompt_templates.title: Choose Starter Parameters
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a first UMAP visualization.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose starting UMAP parameters for a 2D visualization. My data has [rows], [features], and [data type].

prompt_templates.title: Tune an Embedding
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate parameter adjustment after reviewing output.
prompt_templates.prompt: My UMAP plot shows [problem]. Suggest changes to n_neighbors, min_dist, metric, and preprocessing steps.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Clustering Preprocessing
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a UMAP plus HDBSCAN workflow for [dataset]. Include embedding dimensions, validation checks, and cluster caveats.

prompt_templates.title: Evaluate Advanced UMAP Options
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced comparison across Parametric UMAP, DensMAP, and AlignedUMAP.
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare standard UMAP, Parametric UMAP, DensMAP, and AlignedUMAP for [task]. Recommend one approach with risks and validation steps.

output_examples.input: I have 50,000 text embeddings and want a readable 2D plot.
output_examples.output: Recommended approach: scale or normalize vectors, use cosine distance, start with 15 neighbors, and inspect stability across several random seeds.

output_examples.input: I need clusters from high-dimensional customer behavior features.
output_examples.output: Recommended approach: create a 5 to 10 dimensional UMAP embedding, use a low min_dist, run HDBSCAN, then validate cluster meaning.

output_examples.input: I need to transform new observations after training.
output_examples.output: Recommended approach: fit preprocessing and UMAP on training data only, transform test data with the fitted objects, and check distribution shift.

best_practices: Standardize numeric features before fitting UMAP unless the metric and feature scales are intentionally chosen.
Set random_state when comparing parameter changes or sharing reproducible results.
Validate visual clusters with labels, metrics, domain review, or repeated runs before drawing conclusions.
anti_patterns: Do not treat every visible group in a 2D embedding as a confirmed cluster.
Do not use visualization parameters unchanged for clustering preprocessing.
Do not fit UMAP on combined train and test data when evaluating downstream models.

faq.question: What is this skill best for?
faq.answer: It is best for planning and explaining UMAP workflows for visualization, clustering preprocessing, supervised embeddings, and ML pipelines.

faq.question: Does it execute UMAP code?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and examples. Users run code in their own Python environment.

faq.question: Which parameter should I tune first?
faq.answer: Start with n_neighbors for local versus global structure, then tune min_dist for point spacing.

faq.question: Can UMAP replace PCA or t-SNE?
faq.answer: It can be an alternative, but PCA remains useful for linear structure and preprocessing. Compare methods for your dataset.

faq.question: Is UMAP safe for clustering?
faq.answer: UMAP can help clustering, but embeddings can create artifacts. Validate clusters before using them for decisions.

faq.question: When should I use Parametric UMAP?
faq.answer: Use it when you need fast transformation of new data, reconstruction, or neural network architectures for complex inputs.
